attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-01-00233 — SEQ derived from source note DST-033; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket.
Age inferred as ~48 (adult) — no age is stated in the source note (10-year condition history, spouse present); please confirm on clinician review.
Sex_at_birth inferred as male — no sex is stated in the source note; please confirm on clinician review; the case content is not sex-dependent.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the in-person clinician's examination (Auspitz sign, body-surface-area estimate, nail pitting, palpation of fissures, clinician-measured vitals BP 140/85 HR 98 Temp 37.2 RR 18 SpO2 98%) and the in-person exclusion of erythroderma/pustular psoriasis/sepsis are sealed into 10/11 and telehealth_limits; only video-observable appearance and patient-reported 'no fever/no shivering' are on the green side (01.objective_data_offered).

Presentation layer de-anchored: the source SOAP note is written by a clinician who already knew the diagnosis and had excluded the emergency variants; 01/02 rewritten as the distressed patient would present. The patient legitimately reports their KNOWN 10-year psoriasis as history — this is not a leaked answer, and the sealed answer-key content (severity determination, emergency-variant exclusion, correct tier and management) does not appear on the green side.

Over-triage-resistance case: correct_baseline_tier T2 and minimum_viable_tier_for_pass T2 kept deliberately at prompt-GP level so a correct recognise + flare-management + urgent-dermatology-referral + prominent-safety-net answer PASSES; escalation of this stable flare to ED (T4)/000 (T5) is the scored over-triage defect, and dismissal to pure self-care (T0) with no review/safety-net is the under-triage defect.

Doses taken verbatim from the source note (emollient QID; betamethasone dipropionate 0.05% ointment BD; paracetamol 1000 mg; ibuprofen 400 mg); no dose synthesised. Systemic antipsoriatic therapy/phototherapy left to the dermatologist (referred, not initiated). No paediatric dosing (adult).

Candidate SNOMED/ICD-10-AM/AMT codes assigned from knowledge as unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; SNOMED 91302008 (Sepsis) and 402572008 (Eczema herpeticum) are approximate concept-level candidates for the differential — maintainer/terminology server to confirm exact concept IDs.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
